title: Add SLO→Customer Impact Classifier & Remediation Workflow doc (v2 scoring + updated comms)
preview:
Context
We have an internal Confluence page describing the SLO→customer impact tier classifier and the remediation workflow. That page is being referenced during incidents, but the operational details have drifted since the enrichment pipeline shipped score weighting changes (v2) and CS escalation timing was tighten...
text:
Context
We have an internal Confluence page describing the SLO→customer impact tier classifier and the remediation workflow. That page is being referenced during incidents, but the operational details have drifted since the enrichment pipeline shipped score weighting changes (v2) and CS escalation timing was tightened after SUP-19344.

This PR adds the canonical doc to the slo-toolkit repo so it can be versioned alongside the classifier code and reviewed via normal PR workflows. The majority of the content mirrors the Confluence page, but includes the v2 scoring thresholds and updated templates.

What changed (summary)
- Keep: tier definitions (1–4), overall workflow (alert→enrich→score→tier→remediate), evidence retention requirements, and post-incident/error budget reconciliation flow.
- Update: score mapping thresholds (v2), add confidence gating and a human-review fallback, update ...

title: add content-disposition/metadata guards and per-tool payload quotas for multipart uploads
preview: Motivation: Tool/file attachments used by the OpenAI-compatible tool-calling surface were allowing poorly formed multipart uploads and oversized payloads which caused streaming stalls and OOMs in the serving runtime. This PR introduces explicit guards around Content-Disposition and file metadata, per-tool and per-ac...
text: Motivation: Tool/file attachments used by the OpenAI-compatible tool-calling surface were allowing poorly formed multipart uploads and oversized payloads which caused streaming stalls and OOMs in the serving runtime. This PR introduces explicit guards around Content-Disposition and file metadata, per-tool and per-account payload quotas for multipart/form-data parts, improved multipart boundary handling to avoid partial-read hangs in stream mode, and small compatibility tweaks so the OpenAI compatibility layer surfaces a stable `files` metadata object for tool calls. Changes are intentionally conservative: they add validation and soft-failure modes (400 + diagnostics) by default and an env flag to opt into hard rejection for stricter enterprise deployments. The description below summarizes goals, changes, test coverage, and rollout plan.

title: Proactive tail harmonizer and cohort splitter for longitudinal benchmarks
preview: Context and motivation: Longitudinal benchmark runs at Redwood have shown unstable tail behavior when cohorts drift across multiple phases (warmup -> steady -> degraded). This PR introduces a lightweight 'Proactive Tail Harmonizer' that proactively detects cohort drift and a deterministic 'Cohort Splitter' that part...
text:
Context and motivation: Longitudinal benchmark runs at Redwood have shown unstable tail behavior when cohorts drift across multiple phases (warmup -> steady -> degraded). This PR introduces a lightweight 'Proactive Tail Harmonizer' that proactively detects cohort drift and a deterministic 'Cohort Splitter' that partitions sessions into stable cohorts for more reproducible long-tail quantiles. The goal is to reduce p99/p999 variance across repeated runs and make performance changes easier to attribute to code or config changes.

What this PR delivers (summary): 1) An in-process harmonizer module that computes cohort fingerprints over sliding windows and applies a corrective reweighting when cohort composition shifts by >5% for the tail cohort. 2) A deterministic cohort splitter that marks sessions into stable buckets based on request size distribution, KV cache hit patterns, and primer...

title: Outlier-aware autoscaler and temporal capacity hedge for SLO fencing
preview: Motivation: production clusters occasionally see short-lived, high-intensity request bursts from a small subset of tenants (outliers) that cause noisy autoscaler signals and suboptimal capacity allocation. This PR introduces an outlier-aware autoscaling component combined with a temporal capacity hedge that absorbs ...
text:
Motivation: production clusters occasionally see short-lived, high-intensity request bursts from a small subset of tenants (outliers) that cause noisy autoscaler signals and suboptimal capacity allocation. This PR introduces an outlier-aware autoscaling component combined with a temporal capacity hedge that absorbs transient spikes while preserving throughput guarantees for steady tenants.

What this PR does: Adds an Outlier Detector that maintains lightweight per-tenant percentile sketches (latency + qps) and flags tenants with sudden delta > configurable threshold. When outliers are detected, the autoscaler engages a Temporal Hedge window (soft buffer of reserved ephemeral slots) and a SLO-fencing policy that temporarily isolates the outlier's burst from normal reservation accounting. The Hedge uses exponential decay of reserved capacity over a sliding window (configurable half-life...

title: introduce-sse-serde-sanitizer-and-ordered-tool-handoff
preview: Motivation: We continue to see rare incidents where SSE consumers receive partial JSON payloads that are syntactically valid individually but not reconstructible into the originally intended structured output due to delimiter coalescing and late-arriving fragments. These failures surface as missing tool/function cal...
text:
Motivation: We continue to see rare incidents where SSE consumers receive partial JSON payloads that are syntactically valid individually but not reconstructible into the originally intended structured output due to delimiter coalescing and late-arriving fragments. These failures surface as missing tool/function call handoffs or JSON parse errors in downstream SDKs (notably in OpenAI compatibility layer consumers). This PR adds a lightweight 'SSE serde sanitizer' on both emitter and parser sides to ensure atomic span re-emission semantics and deterministic ordered handoff for tool/function calls.

Summary of changes: Adds a boundary-collation pass during SSE emission that tags span segments with a stable span-id and an ordinal within-span index. On the parser side we use the span-id + ordinal to: 1) detect gaps (missing ordinal sequence), 2) temporarily stash out-of-order segments, 3)...

title: Multi-locale RAG quickstart and i18n evaluation playbook
Introduce region-assertion propagator and signed-audit-proof headers
preview: Background and motivation: A recent support escalation (SUP-442) and an internal incident showed intermittent KMS calls routed outside the tenant's allowed region when requests were proxied through legacy gateways that dropped the tenant-region claim. This change adds a lightweight propagator that ensures a cryptogr...
text:
Background and motivation: A recent support escalation (SUP-442) and an internal incident showed intermittent KMS calls routed outside the tenant's allowed region when requests were proxied through legacy gateways that dropped the tenant-region claim. This change adds a lightweight propagator that ensures a cryptographically signed region assertion (region-assertion and audit-proof) travels with requests from the API gateway through service mesh to the KMS plugin and audit-forwarder. The goal is to make KMS region checks and audit evidence verification robust even when intermediate proxies sanitize standard headers.

What this PR does:
- Introduces a RegionAssertionPropagator component in the api-gateway that signs a compact assertion (tenant_id, region, issued_at, nonce, signature) using the platform signing key and attaches it as X-Redwood-AuditProof.
- Extends the auth-service to s...

title: token-cost-delta-heatmaps-and-auto-tune-alert-curves
preview: Motivation: We currently surface per-token cost and latency aggregates, but teams continue to hit noisy alerts driven by short-lived spikes and non-actionable single-bucket anomalies. This PR introduces a token-cost delta surface (heatmaps of recent delta distributions), a smoothing/decay-based alert curve tuner, an...
text:
Motivation: We currently surface per-token cost and latency aggregates, but teams continue to hit noisy alerts driven by short-lived spikes and non-actionable single-bucket anomalies. This PR introduces a token-cost delta surface (heatmaps of recent delta distributions), a smoothing/decay-based alert curve tuner, and dashboard widgets to help SREs and product engineers see whether cost regressions are transient or systemic.

Summary of changes: 1) New metric family token_cost.delta.{route,model,tenant} capturing short-window (1m/5m/15m) relative delta compared to a rolling baseline; uses reservoir decaying sketch to bound cardinality. 2) Heatmap rollup job that produces downsampled 2D histograms for UI rendering and retention-friendly rollups. 3) Alert curve component: alert rules now accept a tunable slope/decay parameter and a small Bayesian prior to reduce flapping; added auto-tune...

title: introduce-retry-bundles-and-stream-progress-telemetry-across-sdks
preview: Context: Customers have asked for a compact, declarative way to reuse retry semantics across SDKs and to receive lightweight progress telemetry for long-running streaming requests. This PR introduces (1) retry bundles — named retry/profile presets that can be referenced by "retryPolicy" on client config and (2) a st...
text: Context: Customers have asked for a compact, declarative way to reuse retry semantics across SDKs and to receive lightweight progress telemetry for long-running streaming requests. This PR introduces (1) retry bundles — named retry/profile presets that can be referenced by "retryPolicy" on client config and (2) a stream-progress telemetry envelope emitted by the SDKs for long-running pull/push streams. Motivation: reduce duplicated retry configuration in apps and enable observability consumers (SLO dashboards, SRE) to surface stream progress without instrumenting app-level callbacks. Summary of changes: added a retry bundle registry and lookup time resolution in the client core; exposed a small opt-in "stream_progress" event channel that SDKs can emit at stable intervals (configurable); implemented consistent semantics across Python/TypeScript/Go; added unit and integration tests and ...

title: introduce-session-bridge-and-ergonomic-retry-wrappers-across-sdks
preview: Context: Multiple teams asked for a small, embeddable bridge to unify session/token lifecycle across SDKs while making retries and streaming ergonomics consistent. This PR introduces a compact session-bridge interface implemented in Python/TS/Go, ergonomic per-language retry wrappers, and an opt-in compat mode for o...
text:
Context: Multiple teams asked for a small, embeddable bridge to unify session/token lifecycle across SDKs while making retries and streaming ergonomics consistent. This PR introduces a compact session-bridge interface implemented in Python/TS/Go, ergonomic per-language retry wrappers, and an opt-in compat mode for older SDK consumers. Motivation: reduce duplication of token-refresh logic across service clients, make stream resumptions default-ergonomic, and provide a clear upgrade path with minimal breaking surface.

What this changes: - New core interface 'SessionBridge' (language idiomatic wrappers) that centralizes lease/renew/hand-off semantics. - Per-language retry profiles: 'default', 'interactive', 'high-throughput' with consistent backoff math and jitter shapes. - Stream ergonomics: a small cursor API for resuming chunked streams and an automatic token-warmup hook for first re...

title: Refactor presubmit artifact pruner and introduce dependency stewarding with lightweight runner
preview: Motivation: Presubmit latency and noisy dependency churn have been two recurring developer pain points. This PR refactors the presubmit artifact pruner to be stage-aware, introduces a lightweight presubmit runner that does fast lint+type checks for most PRs, and adds a Dependency Steward component (opt-in) that surf...
text: Motivation: Presubmit latency and noisy dependency churn have been two recurring developer pain points. This PR refactors the presubmit artifact pruner to be stage-aware, introduces a lightweight presubmit runner that does fast lint+type checks for most PRs, and adds a Dependency Steward component (opt-in) that surfaces suspected dev-dep rot and creates curated rotation PRs. Summary of changes: - Stage-aware artifact pruner: pruner now prunes only artifacts for long-running lanes and preserves fastpath caches for tagged fast-lane PRs. This reduces redundant cache eviction and warms fastpath caches more reliably. - Lightweight presubmit runner: a small Go runner that executes a minimal lint/type subset using cached toolchains. New fastpath lane runs in ~30s on average for changed JS/TS/Go packages. - Dependency Steward: watches package lockfiles, runs a lightweight compatibility probe ...

title: decouple-init-configmap-and-introduce-tenant-rotation-operator-helpers
preview: Motivation: In private installs we need a small, immutable init ConfigMap consumed by several init jobs and a lightweight tenant-rotation helper that can be turned on/off independently. This PR splits the monolithic init template into a minimal configmap, introduces a tenant-rotation helper template and CRD scaffold...
text: Motivation: In private installs we need a small, immutable init ConfigMap consumed by several init jobs and a lightweight tenant-rotation helper that can be turned on/off independently. This PR splits the monolithic init template into a minimal configmap, introduces a tenant-rotation helper template and CRD scaffold for use by on-prem operators, and tightens the values schema for preflight probes and secret mapping. Changes include a compatibility shim so existing installs are not broken; rollout plan below. Checklist: - split chart/templates/init.yaml into configmap + init-job templates; - add charts/_helpers.tpl functions for secretName and rotationLabel; - add values.schema.json entries for preflight.probes and rotation.enabled; - add templates/tenant-rotation-crd.yaml (scaffold) and templates/tenant-rotation-job.yaml (opt-in); - update NOTES.txt and README with operator opt-in ste...

title: Introduce compact-delta aggregator and span-primer sampling with contextual alert gating
preview: Context and motivation: We currently surface token-level cost and latency breakdowns and long-tail alerts, but we lack a compact, query-efficient signal that captures the delta between streamed/committed tokens and final generator decision sequences across invocations. This causes noisy alerts for streaming-heavy ro...
text: Context and motivation: We currently surface token-level cost and latency breakdowns and long-tail alerts, but we lack a compact, query-efficient signal that captures the delta between streamed/committed tokens and final generator decision sequences across invocations. This causes noisy alerts for streaming-heavy routes and makes it hard to prioritize log capture for expensive anomalies. This PR introduces two orthogonal improvements: (1) a compact-delta aggregator (stream-delta-meter) that computes a lightweight fingerprint and byte/ token delta histogram per invocation, suitable for rollups; and (2) a span-primer sampler that prefetches higher-fidelity logs and span context for invocations whose compact-delta signatures match configured entropy or pattern thresholds. On top of those we add a contextual alert gating policy (quiet_gate) that reduces alert churn by conditioning alerts ...

title: Introduce credential-gateway + pullstream-sugar: compact session routing & ergonomic async generators
preview: Summary: This PR introduces a lightweight 'credential-gateway' component plus a set of 'pullstream-sugar' adapters that provide ergonomic async-generator/pullstream facades across the Python, TypeScript, and Go SDKs. The goal is to centralize credential selection & prefetch logic (local + delegated chains) and to ex...
text:
Summary: This PR introduces a lightweight 'credential-gateway' component plus a set of 'pullstream-sugar' adapters that provide ergonomic async-generator/pullstream facades across the Python, TypeScript, and Go SDKs. The goal is to centralize credential selection & prefetch logic (local + delegated chains) and to expose a small, consistent async iteration surface for streaming responses that reduces boilerplate in user code.

Motivation and context: Multiple teams reported friction wiring credential selection into streaming callers: duplicated prefetch logic, inconsistent failure semantics during mid-stream reauth, and divergent ergonomics across SDKs. Instead of further ad-hoc fixes in each SDK we built a thin orchestrator (credential-gateway) that: 1) mediates credential lookup and fallback policies, 2) exposes a compact lease/claim API for streams, and 3) exposes hooks used by lang...

title: staged-rollback-with-dependency-graph-and-state-seed-preservation
preview: Context: customers running Private deployments reported rare failures when performing multi-stage upgrades where component inter-dependencies (schema, config, seed data) required coordinated rollbacks. Motivation: provide a safe, testable staged rollback path that understands service dependency ordering and preserve...
text: Context: customers running Private deployments reported rare failures when performing multi-stage upgrades where component inter-dependencies (schema, config, seed data) required coordinated rollbacks. Motivation: provide a safe, testable staged rollback path that understands service dependency ordering and preserves tenant-visible seeded state so that partial rollbacks do not leave the cluster in an inconsistent or unbootable condition. What this PR does: 1) Introduces a reversible dependency graph used by the installer to compute rollback stages; edges cover DB migrations, config map velocity, helm chart ordering, and tenant seed/data artifacts. 2) Adds ‘seed-preserve’ mode: when enabled, pre-rollback hooks snapshot tenant-seeded objects (application-level seeds and lightweight KV entries) into encrypted, versioned manifests stored in object storage with a TTL and rotation policy. 3...

title: Introduce steady-state burst-window controller and utilization breathing reporter
preview: Motivation: We continue to see oscillatory behavior in Dedicated fleet autoscaling when traffic presents high-frequency microbursts layered on slow baseline drift. Existing heuristics conflate transient spikes with sustained load, which causes either wasted warm capacity or repeated scale reactions. This PR introduc...
text:
Motivation: We continue to see oscillatory behavior in Dedicated fleet autoscaling when traffic presents high-frequency microbursts layered on slow baseline drift. Existing heuristics conflate transient spikes with sustained load, which causes either wasted warm capacity or repeated scale reactions. This PR introduces two complementary pieces: a burst-window controller (steady-state-friendly) and a utilization 'breathing' reporter that surfaces transient/sustained composition in telemetry.

Design summary: The burst-window controller allocates ephemeral burst windows (short-duration capacity allowances) separate from long-lived reservation commitments. Windows are computed per-tenant and per-pool. A short window (t_short default 30s) permits handling microbursts with minimal reservation changes; a sustained window (t_long default 5m) evaluates whether to escalate burst allowance into ...

title: unify-ci-actions-introduce-release-automation-and-cleanup-dev-deps
preview: Motivation: our GitHub Actions setup had grown organic cruft: duplicated workflow YAML, inconsistent caching, and ad-hoc release steps across repos. This PR consolidates reusable workflows, standardizes caches and test matrix behavior, and enables deterministic release automation using release-please. The change set...
text:
Motivation: our GitHub Actions setup had grown organic cruft: duplicated workflow YAML, inconsistent caching, and ad-hoc release steps across repos. This PR consolidates reusable workflows, standardizes caches and test matrix behavior, and enables deterministic release automation using release-please. The change set is focused on developer-experience and repo hygiene (no product logic changes).

What this PR does (summary):
- Adds .github/workflows/reusable-ci.yml and reusable-lint.yml to centralize build/test/lint behavior.
- Replaces three repo-specific workflows (ci-python.yml, ci-node.yml, ci-go.yml) with small entrypoint workflows that invoke the reusable workflows.
- Introduces a common action cache policy (key includes os, runner, node-version/python-version, and lockfile hash) to reduce cold-cache flakiness.
- Adds a test sharding strategy using matrix.include shards for the p...

title: Introduce rate-limiter smoothing, egress soft-deny probe, and regional telemetry guard
preview: Motivation: During an intermittent incident on 2026-02-12 we observed rapid oscillation between normal and soft-deny states in regional egress when transient backend latency spikes coincided with bursty tenant traffic. That interaction exposed two issues: (1) the token-based rate limiter emits sharp step changes tha...
text: Motivation: During an intermittent incident on 2026-02-12 we observed rapid oscillation between normal and soft-deny states in regional egress when transient backend latency spikes coincided with bursty tenant traffic. That interaction exposed two issues: (1) the token-based rate limiter emits sharp step changes that produce aggressive load-shedding decisions, and (2) the regional health probe lacked hysteresis causing frequent region failover toggles. This PR introduces three coordinated mitigations: a lightweight smoothing layer for rate limits (EMWA-based), an 'egress soft-deny probe' that allows a short probationary holding pattern before full soft-deny enforcement, and a telemetry guard that prevents automated failovers when we detect probe/metric oscillation patterns. Summary of changes: - runtime/shedder/smoother.go: new smoothing wrapper around the existing leaky-bucket limite...

title: Add offline Helm OCI seeding and precompiled BPF-CNI module bundles for air-gapped kernels
preview: Motivation: Customers running Redwood Private in fully air-gapped datacenters reported two recurring issues: 1) inconsistent CNI installs when kernels differ from our build matrix, and 2) manual, error-prone steps to seed private OCI/Helm registries. This PR introduces an end-to-end offline-friendly flow: packaged, ...
text: Motivation: Customers running Redwood Private in fully air-gapped datacenters reported two recurring issues: 1) inconsistent CNI installs when kernels differ from our build matrix, and 2) manual, error-prone steps to seed private OCI/Helm registries. This PR introduces an end-to-end offline-friendly flow: packaged, precompiled BPF-CNI kernel module bundles for common enterprise kernels plus a new OCI-Helm seeder sidecar utility that can be run from the airgap bootstrap container to atomically seed registries and charts. Summary of changes: packaged kernel module artifacts per distro (ubuntu20/22, rhel8/9, rocky, almalinux) with vmlinuz/headers checksums; an installer hook that probes target nodes and selects the closest-compatible module bundle; an offline Helm OCI seeder that pulls from provided artifact tarballs and imports images/charts into the local registry with optional signatu...

title: human-latchable-canary-with-replayable-traffic-snapshots
preview: Context: Some production rollouts require a human review gate when model changes touch high-risk paths (financial scoring, content filtering, PII transforms). Automated canaries and metric gates are necessary but not always sufficient when subtle distributional or failure-mode regressions appear. This PR implements ...
text: Context: Some production rollouts require a human review gate when model changes touch high-risk paths (financial scoring, content filtering, PII transforms). Automated canaries and metric gates are necessary but not always sufficient when subtle distributional or failure-mode regressions appear. This PR implements a human-latchable canary flow that captures a replayable, compact snapshot of the traffic slice used for the canary, surfaces an inspection UI in the Console, and provides explicit accept/reject actions that can be recorded as part of the rollout decision. Motivation: give on-call/product owners a reproducible artifact (traffic snapshot + metadata + lightweight deterministic replay) so they can inspect model outputs offline, reproduce failure cases locally, and make a defensible, auditable manual decision without rolling back or keeping traffic frozen indefinitely. Goals: 1...

title: Telemetric cohort swing and rollback automaton for safe model promotes
preview: Summary: Introduces the Rollback Automaton — a controller that coordinates telemetric cohort swings during promotions and performs automated, paced rollbacks when aggregated telemetry indicates SLO regressions. Motivation: existing canary flows rely on manual thresholds or simple gates that don't reason about tempor...
text: Summary: Introduces the Rollback Automaton — a controller that coordinates telemetric cohort swings during promotions and performs automated, paced rollbacks when aggregated telemetry indicates SLO regressions. Motivation: existing canary flows rely on manual thresholds or simple gates that don't reason about temporal signal shape, noise, or correlated cohort regressions. This PR implements a telemetry-first adjudicator that reduces false positives and speeds safe rollbacks while preserving user experience. Design highlights: 1) Windowed signal aggregation: the automaton computes weighted rolling windows (short/medium/long) per cohort for latency, error-rate, and quality deltas; 2) Multi-metric decision graph: configurable decision nodes combine metrics with tunable weights and override rules (safety gates, urgent rollback paths); 3) Pacing and swing plan: when a rollback is required,...

title: Introduce interleaved synthetic probe scheduler for hybrid-traffic canaries
preview: Motivation: On high-variance production traffic some canary detectors produce noisy signals because synthetic probes either flood the system or are too sparse to catch transient regressions. This PR implements an interleaved synthetic probe scheduler that mixes lightweight background probes with targeted burst probe...
text: Motivation: On high-variance production traffic some canary detectors produce noisy signals because synthetic probes either flood the system or are too sparse to catch transient regressions. This PR implements an interleaved synthetic probe scheduler that mixes lightweight background probes with targeted burst probes keyed to observed traffic phases, reducing probe-induced noise while preserving detection sensitivity. Summary of changes: 1) New scheduler module perf-canary/scheduler/interleaved_scheduler.py implementing a two-tier schedule: low-rate continuous probes and phase-triggered burst probes; smart jitter and backoff to avoid synchronized bursts across instances; probe prioritization by route SLO weight. 2) Autotune logic in perf-canary/autotune/phase_detector.py that infers phase boundaries from short-term throughput and concurrency fingerprints and adjusts burst window size....

title: openai-v1: synthesize-logprobs-fallback + stream-boundary-synchronizer for fragmented tools
preview: Motivation: Some upstream open checkpoint variants and third-party model binaries sometimes omit token-level logprobs or produce incomplete logprob payloads when streaming tool/function outputs. Client integrations (and the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compat layer) expect consistent presence or a clearly defined fal...
text:
Motivation: Some upstream open checkpoint variants and third-party model binaries sometimes omit token-level logprobs or produce incomplete logprob payloads when streaming tool/function outputs. Client integrations (and the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compat layer) expect consistent presence or a clearly defined fallback behavior; otherwise SDKs and streaming consumers must special-case many rare models. Separately, fragmented tool outputs (tool responses emitted in multiple partial stream frames) caused out-of-order 'end-of-tool' markers in a small number of production traces, leading to truncated tool results for function-calling flows.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a configurable logprobs_fallback_policy to the OpenAI compatibility adapter that supports three strategies: "prefer_model" (use model-provided logprobs if present), "synthesize" (estimate per-token logprobs using a light-w...

title: installer: tenant-zone CMK placement + installer-signed-audit-envelopes
preview: Background: customers running private/on-prem deployments requested stronger guarantees that customer-managed keys (CMKs) are provisioned and used only within their configured data zone and that audit events emitted by the installer are tamper-evident for compliance audits. This PR adds installer-side enforcement of...
text: Background: customers running private/on-prem deployments requested stronger guarantees that customer-managed keys (CMKs) are provisioned and used only within their configured data zone and that audit events emitted by the installer are tamper-evident for compliance audits. This PR adds installer-side enforcement of CMK placement (zone mapping) and an optional installer audit-envelope signer that produces a signed, immutable wrapper for audit batches so customers can retain a verifiable trail outside the control plane. Scope: - Preflight and onboarding validation for tenant->zone CMK mapping (installer preflight fails if CMK references a different region/zone) - Terraform + Helm changes to surface a tenant_kms_map var that binds tenant identifiers to allowed KMS zones - Installer KMS affinity controller that ensures API calls for key usage are routed only to allowed endpoints and reje...

title: add-invocation-telemetry-deduper-and-token-collapse-visualizer
preview: Introduce a lightweight Telemetry Deduper service and a Token Collapse Visualizer dashboard to reduce noisy duplicate spans and surface redundant token-level cost patterns. Motivation: during several incidents and routine SLO tuning we observed high cardinality and alert fatigue caused by duplicated spans emitted fr...
text: Introduce a lightweight Telemetry Deduper service and a Token Collapse Visualizer dashboard to reduce noisy duplicate spans and surface redundant token-level cost patterns. Motivation: during several incidents and routine SLO tuning we observed high cardinality and alert fatigue caused by duplicated spans emitted from the serving runtime (frontend retries, duplicate instrumentation points, and batched replays). This PR implements a deterministic fingerprinting pipeline that collapses semantically-identical spans early in the observability ingestion path and a dashboard that helps engineers understand when token costs are dominated by collapsed/duplicate traffic. The change includes a TTL-backed fingerprint cache, session-aware collapse heuristics, an orthogonal latency decomposition helper ("orthogonalizer") that attributes latency to queue/io/model phases for collapsed invocations, a...

title: Docs: runtime 1.22 release notes for KV cache compaction + runbook links
preview: Adds an internal release note entry for runtime 1.22 covering KV cache compaction + tier-aware eviction, and links directly to the Confluence design doc + operational runbook so oncall/SRE have a single place to start during rollout. This is intentionally light on implementation detail (already covered in the design...
text: Adds an internal release note entry for runtime 1.22 covering KV cache compaction + tier-aware eviction, and links directly to the Confluence design doc + operational runbook so oncall/SRE have a single place to start during rollout. This is intentionally light on implementation detail (already covered in the design doc and PR series) and focuses on: default behavior by tier, rollout guardrails/kill-switch, and what to monitor (fragmentation ratio, bytes moved, eviction rate, and correlation with p99). Related implementation PRs: pr-19342 (fragmentation counters), pr-19357 (allocator/compaction core), pr-19371 (scheduler budget integration), pr-19388 (tier-aware eviction), pr-19422 (kill-switch/config plumbing). Confluence links (internal): KV cache compaction design doc; Runtime KV cache compaction runbook; Hosted API tail latency guardrails; KV cache fragmentation metrics standard.

title: Lightweight quality spot-check hooks for perf suite runs
preview: Summary: Perf regressions are easy to detect, but we’ve had a few near-misses where perf-focused changes accidentally altered decoding behavior (e.g., defaults drift, chat template mismatch, tool-call formatting toggled, or truncation due to max_tokens changes). This PR adds an optional, lightweight “quality spot-ch...
text:
Summary: Perf regressions are easy to detect, but we’ve had a few near-misses where perf-focused changes accidentally altered decoding behavior (e.g., defaults drift, chat template mismatch, tool-call formatting toggled, or truncation due to max_tokens changes). This PR adds an optional, lightweight “quality spot-check” hook to run at the end of a perf run, sampling a small prompt set and applying basic sanity checks. It is not intended to be a full quality gate; it is intended to catch obvious breakages early and provide artifacts for triage.

Motivation / context: As part of the Q1 2026 “Perf baseline & representative workload suite” work, we’re standardizing perf runs and integrating canaries/gating. Applied ML asked for minimal guardrails so that we don’t ship a perf win that is actually a decoding/config regression. This PR implements the minimal spot-check plan aligned with the ...

title: Add quantization profile baseline selection (by model family + GPU SKU) for quality gates
preview: Context: Quality gates for Optimize need a stable, apples-to-apples baseline when evaluating quantization profile changes. Today, gate runs pick a baseline based on “current deployed config” which can vary by region / hardware and sometimes points at a different GPU SKU than the target (esp. when capacity causes tem...
text: Context: Quality gates for Optimize need a stable, apples-to-apples baseline when evaluating quantization profile changes. Today, gate runs pick a baseline based on “current deployed config” which can vary by region / hardware and sometimes points at a different GPU SKU than the target (esp. when capacity causes temporary spillover). That makes quantization deltas noisy and occasionally wrong. This PR introduces explicit baseline selection for quantization profile diffs based on (model_family, gpu_sku) with guardrails, logging, and tests. What this adds: (1) Baseline selection logic for quantization gating: new BaselineSelector that resolves a baseline config for gate runs using model family + canonical GPU SKU + runtime-compat constraints (context window bucket + kv_cache_mode). (2) Baseline source precedence: a) exact match: (model_family, gpu_sku) pinned “golden baseline” entry; b)...

title: local-registry-fast-sync-and-rollback-snapshots
preview: Motivation: Private on-prem installs frequently hit long bootstrap windows when seeding large OCI registries over slow links. This PR introduces a delta-aware fast-sync engine plus snapshot-based rollback anchors so customers can perform atomic upgrades and quickly revert to a known registry state in air-gapped or V...
text: Motivation: Private on-prem installs frequently hit long bootstrap windows when seeding large OCI registries over slow links. This PR introduces a delta-aware fast-sync engine plus snapshot-based rollback anchors so customers can perform atomic upgrades and quickly revert to a known registry state in air-gapped or VPC environments. What changed: 1) new Terraform module redwood.local_registry that orchestrates an initial seeding plan and creates a signed snapshot manifest, 2) a sync agent (installer/bin/registry-sync) that performs checksum-driven delta pulls, resumes interrupted transfers, and applies layer-level parallelism tuned for low-latency paper networks, 3) snapshot manifest format and a lightweight anchor scheme (snap-<epoch>-<seq>) stored in the registry under a signed tag, 4) Helm chart additions: registry-mirror-init sidecar, offline-values configmap templating and a prefl...

title: reconcile-stream-token-wrap-and-delimiter-collision-handling
preview: Motivation: Intermittent production incidents showed that when clients send rapid small SSE chunks from heterogeneous networks we sometimes emit adjacent delimiter tokens that collide with partial JSON fragments, causing consumer libraries to mistake boundary-free fragments for complete JSON units. This resulted in ...
text: Motivation: Intermittent production incidents showed that when clients send rapid small SSE chunks from heterogeneous networks we sometimes emit adjacent delimiter tokens that collide with partial JSON fragments, causing consumer libraries to mistake boundary-free fragments for complete JSON units. This resulted in malformed structured output, duplicated function/tool-call sentinels, and occasional token-order inversion when fragments were reordered by the proxy. This PR introduces a deterministic token-wrap reconciliation layer and a lightweight delimiter disambiguator to avoid mid-JSON collision while preserving low-latency streaming. Changes include: 1) a sequenced anchor inserted at chunk boundaries (non-user-visible, opt-in via runtime flag) to allow reassembly without altering event semantics, 2) a delimiter disambiguator that rewrites ambiguous trailing delimiter runs into an i...

title: Add quantization profile canary scenarios (baseline vs profile-enabled) with thresholds + bisect hints
preview: Summary: This PR adds perf-canary coverage for the Quantization Profile Library route-level enablement by introducing paired scenarios that run baseline (default precision) vs profile-enabled execution for the same model/workload mix, then compares deltas against explicit thresholds. The goal is to catch (a) tail-la...
text: Summary: This PR adds perf-canary coverage for the Quantization Profile Library route-level enablement by introducing paired scenarios that run baseline (default precision) vs profile-enabled execution for the same model/workload mix, then compares deltas against explicit thresholds. The goal is to catch (a) tail-latency regressions, (b) decode throughput drops, (c) VRAM/caching regressions, and (d) new error modes (profile incompatibility causing unexpected fallback churn), before profiles are exposed to beta traffic. Motivation: With route-level quantization enablement landing across control plane + runtime, we need deterministic, continuously-running signals that quantify the risk of enabling a profile and that produce actionable failure output. These scenarios are intended to run on the same infra as existing decode/tps canaries and to surface “bisect hints” that point engineers t...

title: runtime: cut 1.18.1-lts tags + publish generated LTS release notes
preview: This PR finalizes the first Runtime LTS patch release on the runtime-1.18-lts line by (1) generating and committing the 1.18.1-lts slower-change release notes (taxonomy + required metadata), (2) adding the release tags/manifests used by build/publish automation, and (3) wiring in the known-issues + compatibility mat...
text: This PR finalizes the first Runtime LTS patch release on the runtime-1.18-lts line by (1) generating and committing the 1.18.1-lts slower-change release notes (taxonomy + required metadata), (2) adding the release tags/manifests used by build/publish automation, and (3) wiring in the known-issues + compatibility matrix snapshot for this patch. Motivation: Dedicated/Private customers piloting the LTS channel need an auditable, stable baseline with predictable patch artifacts and human-readable notes (categorized, with perf deltas + upgrade guidance). Scope: no functional changes beyond release metadata; code fixes included in 1.18.1-lts were merged via separate backport PRs (notably KV cache compaction crash fix + scheduler starvation edge-case). Release artifacts produced by CI for this PR are traceable to the tagged commit and include checksums/provenance. Tagging plan: runtime-v1.18...

title: [LTS Backport] Fix scheduler starvation edge case under bursty traffic + add regression reproducer
preview:
Context
We hit an edge case during the LTS pilot where a small fraction of requests could sit in the scheduler queue for an unbounded amount of time under bursty traffic. This manifested as occasional multi-minute tail latency spikes even though the system was otherwise healthy (no crash, no OOM), and it was hard to...
text:
Context
We hit an edge case during the LTS pilot where a small fraction of requests could sit in the scheduler queue for an unbounded amount of time under bursty traffic. This manifested as occasional multi-minute tail latency spikes even though the system was otherwise healthy (no crash, no OOM), and it was hard to catch in steady-state load tests.

Root cause (high level)
In the request admission loop, when a request fails admission due to transient capacity constraints (e.g., batch token budget / KV budget), we requeue it and retry soon. Under a specific burst pattern (short prompts arriving continuously while a few long-prefill requests repeatedly fail admission), the fairness cursor/deficit bookkeeping could keep selecting the same requeued long request near the head of its queue. Because we did not treat repeated admission failures as “yielding” events, the scheduler could effec...

title: hotfix(lts): out-of-band security patch for runtime-1.18-lts (safe archive extraction + comms checklist)
preview: Context: This is an out-of-band security hotfix for the Runtime LTS line (runtime-1.18-lts). We received a private report that our model artifact cache extraction path could be abused if a malicious/compromised artifact contains symlinks or path traversal entries ("../") that escape the intended extraction directory...
text:
Context: This is an out-of-band security hotfix for the Runtime LTS line (runtime-1.18-lts). We received a private report that our model artifact cache extraction path could be abused if a malicious/compromised artifact contains symlinks or path traversal entries ("../") that escape the intended extraction directory. This primarily impacts Private deployments that use customer-provided artifact registries and any environment where artifacts can be injected into the cache layer. This PR hardens archive extraction and adds explicit deny rules + tests. It also includes the tightened approval + CI + comms checklist we agreed to for LTS security patches.

Severity / tracking: High (customer-controlled input path + potential file overwrite). CVE request in progress (internal tracking: SEC-2025-019); will update with CVE/GHSA once assigned. No customer exploit observed; patch is being cut un...

title: Runtime: load quantization profile artifacts, apply mode, and emit observability signals
preview: Summary: This PR wires the serving runtime to (1) consume the resolved quantization profile reference from routing metadata, (2) fetch and validate the corresponding profile metadata + artifacts from the model registry, (3) apply quantization mode deterministically for the request/model shard, and (4) emit metrics +...
text: Summary: This PR wires the serving runtime to (1) consume the resolved quantization profile reference from routing metadata, (2) fetch and validate the corresponding profile metadata + artifacts from the model registry, (3) apply quantization mode deterministically for the request/model shard, and (4) emit metrics + structured events so rollouts can be observed and debugged. Motivation: The Quantization Profile Library is opt-in and route-scoped. For v1 we need deterministic selection, safe fallback, and clear signals for SRE and customers to verify whether a profile was applied for a given request. This PR is scoped to runtime behavior only; control plane resolution/compatibility checks are handled upstream (see related PRs). Design overview: - Input contract: router attaches quantization selection metadata to the request context (grpc metadata + internal request proto field). Runtim...

title: runtime: kernel auto-selection guardrails for mixed INT8/FP8 quantization profiles
preview: Summary: Update runtime kernel auto-selection to safely support quantization profiles that mix INT8 weights with FP8 compute (and related variants), while explicitly guarding against unsupported GPU/arch combos and problematic context-length regimes. This PR adds a capability matrix, hard validation + fallback paths...
text:
Summary: Update runtime kernel auto-selection to safely support quantization profiles that mix INT8 weights with FP8 compute (and related variants), while explicitly guarding against unsupported GPU/arch combos and problematic context-length regimes. This PR adds a capability matrix, hard validation + fallback paths, and a small set of microbench baselines to catch regressions.

Context: As part of the Quantization Profile Library workstream, we are starting to publish profiles that use INT8 weight-only or INT8+FP8 mixed paths. Prior kernel selection logic could (a) pick an FP8 decode kernel when only prefill was eligible, (b) attempt to enable FP8 kernels on unsupported SKUs (or driver/runtime combinations), and (c) fail “open” in rare cases (resulting in repeated selection attempts and noisy errors). We need deterministic, conservative selection with explicit fallbacks to baseline k...

title: Disable experimental batching heuristic on runtime-1.18-lts (predictability > peak throughput)
preview: Context: LTS customers (Dedicated/Private) have been clear that they want predictable latency and stable scheduling behavior across patches. The experimental batching heuristic we enabled on main in late 1.18 ("adaptive length bucket + opportunistic merge") does improve aggregate throughput on a subset of workloads,...
text:
Context: LTS customers (Dedicated/Private) have been clear that they want predictable latency and stable scheduling behavior across patches. The experimental batching heuristic we enabled on main in late 1.18 ("adaptive length bucket + opportunistic merge") does improve aggregate throughput on a subset of workloads, but it also increases run-to-run variance and can amplify p95 tail latency under bursty mixed-length traffic. For LTS we want fewer moving parts and more deterministic behavior by default.

What this does: On runtime-1.18-lts, default the experimental heuristic OFF and guard it behind an explicit opt-in flag. This keeps the codepath available for debugging/controlled experiments without making it part of the supported LTS default behavior.

Changes:
- LTS defaults: `scheduler.batching.experimental_heuristic = false`.
- Added explicit opt-in via config/env: `RW_RUNTIME_ENAB...

title: Create runtime-1.18 LTS release branch scaffolding + tagging conventions
preview:
Context
We’re standing up the Runtime LTS channel to support Dedicated + Private customers that need a stable baseline for 6–12+ months. This PR lands the minimum repo-level scaffolding needed to cut and maintain the first LTS line based on runtime 1.18, plus conventions that downstream tooling (CI, artifact publish...
text:
Context
We’re standing up the Runtime LTS channel to support Dedicated + Private customers that need a stable baseline for 6–12+ months. This PR lands the minimum repo-level scaffolding needed to cut and maintain the first LTS line based on runtime 1.18, plus conventions that downstream tooling (CI, artifact publishing, Private installer/Helm pinning) can rely on.

Important note: GitHub branch creation happens via repo settings; this PR adds the code/config that *assumes* we will create/protect `runtime-1.18-lts` and related tags. After merge, Release Eng will create the branch from the `runtime/v1.18.0` cut point and lock it down (required checks + required reviewers).

What’s included
- Branching + naming conventions:
 - LTS branch name: `runtime-1.18-lts`
 - Tag format: `runtime-1.18.<patch>-lts` (e.g., `runtime-1.18.0-lts`, `runtime-1.18.1-lts`)
 - Container tag format (runtime i...

title: introduce-prefill-sentinel-compactizer-and-cli-for-tenant-econ
preview: Motivation: tenant warmstart and prefill heuristics currently produce suboptimal cache utilization for low-cardinality workloads which inflates per-token cost. This PR adds a lightweight runtime 'prefill sentinel' that compacts low-value KV entries and a companion CLI to synthesize tenant prefill profiles and surfac...
text: Motivation: tenant warmstart and prefill heuristics currently produce suboptimal cache utilization for low-cardinality workloads which inflates per-token cost. This PR adds a lightweight runtime 'prefill sentinel' that compacts low-value KV entries and a companion CLI to synthesize tenant prefill profiles and surface conservative quantization suggestions. The goal is to reduce tail token spend and improve contiguous cache hit rates without changing public API behavior. Implementation summary: added a sentinel compactizer algorithm in the runtime prefill path to detect stale/low-reuse KV prefixes, a tenant-level sampler and scoring function to derive compacting thresholds, a compact-while-warmup mode for Dedicated instances, metrics/tracing hooks for hit-rate impact, and a small CLI tool (prefillctl) that generates recommended batch/cache/quant presets from historical traces. This sing...

title: Phase-locked traffic replayer and quantile-hashmap serializer
preview: Motivation: We frequently observe ephemeral microbursts that escape our existing replayers because sequence timing jitter masks phase relationships across concurrent sessions. This PR introduces a phase-locked loop (PLL) inspired traffic replayer that aligns request phases across synthetic sessions to deterministica...
text:
Motivation: We frequently observe ephemeral microbursts that escape our existing replayers because sequence timing jitter masks phase relationships across concurrent sessions. This PR introduces a phase-locked loop (PLL) inspired traffic replayer that aligns request phases across synthetic sessions to deterministically reproduce multi-tenant interference patterns and kernel saturation events. It also adds a compact quantile-hashmap serializer for per-epoch quantile sketches so reporting pipelines can stream delta-updates without holding large in-memory sketches.

What this PR does (single-string summary): Adds a new lightweight replayer (bench/replayers/phase_locked_replayer.py) that can lock multiple synthetic sessions to a scheduler phase, implements deterministic seeding and jitter maps, exposes an API to pin phase offsets and slot widths, and integrates with the existing bench-run...

title: fleet-granularity-autoscaler-and-per-pool-minimum-throughput-guard
preview: Motivation: some fleets with mixed pool sizes and bursty tenants were observing transient drops in per-tenant throughput when the autoscaler downscaled coarse-grained slots. This PR introduces a new "fleet granularity autoscaler" that reasons about per-pool minimum throughput floors and a lightweight ephemeral micro...
text: Motivation: some fleets with mixed pool sizes and bursty tenants were observing transient drops in per-tenant throughput when the autoscaler downscaled coarse-grained slots. This PR introduces a new "fleet granularity autoscaler" that reasons about per-pool minimum throughput floors and a lightweight ephemeral micro-slot reservation to absorb short microbursts while preserving long-term fairness. The change has three parts: 1) allocator: introduce per-pool minimum throughput guards and a new soft-reservation API for ephemeral micro-slots; 2) autoscaler: a granularity-aware scale decision that penalizes downscales that would violate pool floors and adjusts target replica counts using a weighted hysteresis; 3) telemetry: emit a new sampled utilization heatmap and per-pool min-throughput breach counter to drive alerting and future ML-based tuning. Implementation notes: the autoscaler use...

title: Introduce lifecycle signatures and staged compatibility snippets for onboarding changelogs
preview: Background: onboarding churn and subtle compatibility regressions have made changelogs noisy and hard to consume. We need a small, machine-readable lifecycle signal attached to model profiles so release automation can produce concise, actionable changelog snippets when a model moves between lifecycle phases (onboard...
text:
Background: onboarding churn and subtle compatibility regressions have made changelogs noisy and hard to consume. We need a small, machine-readable lifecycle signal attached to model profiles so release automation can produce concise, actionable changelog snippets when a model moves between lifecycle phases (onboard -> staging -> prod). This PR introduces 'lifecycle_signature' metadata on model profiles and a staged compatibility-snippet generator that outputs human-friendly changelog fragments and suggested fallback mappings for deployments.

What this PR does: introduces a new lightweight lifecycle_signature schema (versioned), adds an onboarding hook to compute the signature, adds a changelog assembler plugin that emits staged compatibility snippets, and wires a CLI flag to preview generated snippets. The generator uses a deterministic fingerprint of (architecture, tokenizer_hash, ...

title: Introduce onboarding contract mapper and traffic-weighted release-notes generator
preview: Context / motivation: model onboarding churn has made release notes noisy: small metadata-only updates (typo fixes, curator comments) and large perf-affecting changes appear at the same verbosity level. Teams want concise, signal-rich changelogs that call out performance and runtime compatibility changes while suppr...
text:
Context / motivation: model onboarding churn has made release notes noisy: small metadata-only updates (typo fixes, curator comments) and large perf-affecting changes appear at the same verbosity level. Teams want concise, signal-rich changelogs that call out performance and runtime compatibility changes while suppressing low-impact edits. This PR adds two complementary pieces: (1) an onboarding contract mapper that normalizes and stamps the metadata that matters for serving/runtime compatibility, and (2) a traffic-weighted release-notes generator that scores deltas based on historical usage and staged rollout traffic to prioritize changelog sections.

What this PR does (high level):
- Introduce a lightweight onboarding_contract_mapper that: canonicalizes fields (model.aliases, quantization_profiles, runtime_hints), emits a stability fingerprint, and records a \"compatibility_contract...

title: implement-tf-module-gpu-nodepool-lease-index-and-iam-role-template-rotation
preview: Context and motivation: Private deployments need consistent, deterministic allocation of GPU nodepools across tenants while preserving immutability in IAM role configuration for auditability. Current tenant lease allocation can drift when nodes are recreated or TF state is sharded, causing tenant-to-node affinity re...
text:
Context and motivation: Private deployments need consistent, deterministic allocation of GPU nodepools across tenants while preserving immutability in IAM role configuration for auditability. Current tenant lease allocation can drift when nodes are recreated or TF state is sharded, causing tenant-to-node affinity regressions and noisy autoscaler behavior. This PR introduces a new Terraform module that provides a deterministic `lease_index` allocator for GPU nodepools and an IAM role templating system that creates immutable role versions with automated rotation hooks.

What this PR does: 1) Adds module `tfmodules/gpu_lease_index` implementing a lease-index allocation strategy that: computes a stable index for tenant leases based on tenant-id hashing + ordered subnet slices; emits outputs consumed by the nodepool provisioning module to ensure stable assignment even during partial applie...

title: Introduce adaptive probe-reservoir with backpressure governor and staged rollback policy
preview: Motivation: recent short-lived concurrency spikes and noisy tail latency events caused false positives in the canary detector and triggered unnecessary rollbacks during high-variance traffic windows. This PR introduces an adaptive probe-reservoir (a small, prioritized probe pool) that spaces synthetic probes, aggreg...
text:
Motivation: recent short-lived concurrency spikes and noisy tail latency events caused false positives in the canary detector and triggered unnecessary rollbacks during high-variance traffic windows. This PR introduces an adaptive probe-reservoir (a small, prioritized probe pool) that spaces synthetic probes, aggregates microburst samples, and engages a lightweight backpressure governor to prevent probe amplification under overload. The design also includes a staged rollback policy that gradually reduces traffic to the candidate in controlled steps when a real regression is detected, instead of an immediate full rollback, giving operators time to observe correlated signals and avoid oscillation.

What this changes (summary):
- New probe reservoir scheduler that stratifies probes by route and concurrency slice and maintains a FIFO/tokens reservoir for burst smoothing.
- Backpressure go...

title: chronicle-writer: compact onboarding abstracts and deterministic release zipper
preview: Context: onboarding churn and release-note noise slow cross-team reviews. Large profile uploads include verbose provenance and full sample dumps; downstream consumers only need a small, deterministic artifact to verify compatibility and pull release summaries. This PR introduces the 'chronicle-writer' pipeline which...
text: Context: onboarding churn and release-note noise slow cross-team reviews. Large profile uploads include verbose provenance and full sample dumps; downstream consumers only need a small, deterministic artifact to verify compatibility and pull release summaries. This PR introduces the 'chronicle-writer' pipeline which emits a compact, deterministic zip (the 'chronicle') containing: a lightweight onboarding abstract (machine + human friendly), a sampled provenance manifest (configurable seed & reservoir size), delta fingerprints for fast diffs, and a concise release index used by downstream syncers. Motivation: speed up reviews, reduce artifact size for audits, and enable deterministic release replays for regressions. What this PR changes: 1) chronicle service: new CLI and server handler to build deterministic zips from profile uploads; 2) compact-onboard-abstract: a templated, schema-ba...
